observations.what_customers_say: Some customers describe frustration with delays, missing components, or slow project movement after signing.
observations.decision_relevance: A prospect may hesitate before signing if they fear that the contract starts a long opaque process.

observations.what_customers_say: Customers can lose trust when callbacks, status updates, or responsibility are unclear.
observations.decision_relevance: Before signing, customers may want to know who owns issues and how support works.

observations.what_customers_say: Some negative reviews and responses center on what happens when project expectations are missed.
observations.decision_relevance: Customers may care about escalation paths and realistic promises as much as the initial quote.

observations.what_customers_say: Post-contract complaints can become pre-contract concerns for new buyers reading reviews.
observations.decision_relevance: The buyer may need credible process detail before they believe the offer can be delivered.

observations.what_customers_say: Company responses frame some complaints as process or installation issues.
observations.decision_relevance: Public responses can reduce or increase confidence depending on how specific and accountable they are.

cross_source_notes: Delay and support concerns also appear in Svea Solar and consumer-complaint sources.
This source is more useful for fulfillment-risk patterns than for quote-stage motivations.
caveats: Negative reviews can overrepresent unresolved problems.
Some visible complaint details may be incomplete without expanded review text.
Provider-specific complaints should not be used as claims about another installer.
